github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGenerat.text: Generate exactly one commit subject line using Conventional Commits: <type>(<optional-scope>): <description>.
Allowed types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert.
Keep the subject concise: 8-14 words, present tense, no ending period.
Describe only observable code/config/file changes; do not explain intent, impact, or motivation.
Mention 1-2 most important changes only; avoid long lists and filler words.
If scope is clear, include it (for example: feat(shared): ..., fix(desktop): ...).
If CHANGELOG.md or changelog files are updated, align wording with their updated entry.
If changelog entries include issue links, append the related issue number reference in subject (for example: (#123)).
Output subject only. No body, no footer, no code block, no quotes.
Good: feat(shared): add SingleEvent and update event API
Bad: feat: add event handling improvements for better management and future extensibility
